briefing_memo: McKinsey & Company Canada has received federal contracts totaling $209 million to date, 70% of which were awarded non-competitively in frequent disregard of procurement policies according to the Auditor General (June 2024). The firm currently faces a certified Canada-wide class action lawsuit alleging it fueled the opioid epidemic through consulting work that promoted opioid over-prescription for Purdue Pharma and other pharmaceutical manufacturers (B.C. court certification April 2024). McKinsey & Company, Inc. entered into a Deferred Prosecution Agreement with U.S. authorities in February 2025, committing to maintain an effective compliance program and internal controls, while the Canadian government implemented mandatory procurement procedures requiring manager certifications on conflict of interest following the 2024 audit. Given the active litigation, recent U.S. deferred prosecution, and systemic procurement violations, recommend enhanced due diligence and competitive procurement requirements for any new McKinsey contracts, with quarterly reporting to Treasury Board on compliance and value demonstration until the class action resolves and the DPA term concludes.

actions.title: Conduct enhanced due diligence review of ongoing contracts
actions.rationale: Recent certification of Canada-wide class action regarding opioid crisis involvement warrants verification that U.S. Deferred Prosecution Agreement (Feb 2025) remediation measures apply to Canadian operations and that integrity safeguards are in place.

actions.title: Request written confirmation of DPA compliance and remediation implementation
actions.rationale: U.S. Deferred Prosecution Agreement signed February 2025 indicates material misconduct in pharmaceutical consulting; confirm remediation protocols extend to Canadian entity and verify monitoring obligations are being met.

actions.title: Track class action litigation outcome and any findings of wrongdoing
actions.rationale: Canada-wide class action certified in April 2024 remains active; monitor for settlement, judgment, or adverse findings that would impact supplier integrity standing.
